name: Michigan TCF Center
description: The TCF Center, formerly known as Cobo Center, is a large convention center located in Detroit, Michigan. Conspiracy theories surrounding the TCF Center primarily emerged during and after the 2020 United States presidential election. These theories generally allege that the TCF Center was the site of widespread voter fraud, specifically focusing on absentee ballots. Proponents often claim that irregularities in vote counts, alleged blocked access for Republican poll watchers, and supposed unusual activity within the center point to a coordinated effort to manipulate the election results in favor of Joe Biden. Evidence cited often includes selectively edited video footage, anecdotal accounts from individuals claiming to have witnessed suspicious activity, and statistical anomalies presented without proper context. Mainstream perspectives, including multiple court rulings and official investigations, have consistently debunked these claims. Election officials and independent fact-checkers have demonstrated that standard procedures were followed, poll watchers were present, and vote counts were accurate. The alleged statistical anomalies have been explained by factors such as demographic shifts, increased absentee voting due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and the specific rules governing ballot processing in Michigan. The spread of these theories was fueled by partisan media outlets and social media platforms, contributing to widespread distrust in the electoral process. Despite numerous audits and recounts confirming the accuracy of the election results, some individuals continue to believe in these conspiracy theories, highlighting the challenges of combating misinformation in the digital age. The TCF Center continues to operate as a convention center, hosting various events and serving as a significant economic hub for the city of Detroit.
subtitle: Site of 2020 election fraud conspiracies

claims.claim: Widespread voter fraud occurred at the TCF Center during the 2020 election.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts of poll watchers being denied access, statistical anomalies in vote counts, and selectively edited video footage.
claims.criticism: Multiple court rulings, official investigations, and recounts have found no evidence of widespread voter fraud. Allegations of denied access have been refuted by election officials, and statistical anomalies have been explained by legitimate factors such as increased absentee voting.

claims.claim: Absentee ballots were illegally processed and counted at the TCF Center.
claims.evidence: Claims of ballots being counted without proper verification or signature matching, and allegations of ballots being brought in late at night.
claims.criticism: Election officials have stated that standard procedures for verifying and counting absentee ballots were followed. Allegations of late-night ballot deliveries have been debunked, and any discrepancies have been attributed to normal logistical challenges.

claims.claim: Dominion voting machines at the TCF Center were manipulated to alter vote totals.
claims.evidence: Claims of software glitches or backdoors in the Dominion voting system, and allegations of the machines being connected to the internet.
claims.criticism: Audits and forensic investigations of Dominion voting machines have found no evidence of manipulation or security breaches. Claims of internet connectivity have been debunked by election officials and cybersecurity experts.

keyFigures.name: Rudy Giuliani
keyFigures.role: Promoted election fraud claims related to the TCF Center.

keyFigures.name: Sidney Powell
keyFigures.role: Promoted election fraud claims related to the TCF Center.

keyFigures.name: Jocelyn Benson
keyFigures.role: Michigan Secretary of State, defended the integrity of the election.

mythologyConnections: The idea of a 'stolen election' resonates with themes of betrayal and injustice in mythology.
Conspiracy theories often create a narrative of good vs. evil, similar to mythological battles.

timeline.event: Vote counting at the TCF Center becomes a focal point of election fraud allegations.
Numerous lawsuits filed challenging the election results in Michigan, all of which were dismissed or withdrawn.
Conspiracy theories surrounding the TCF Center continue to circulate online.
